F4.evidence: doctor.ts 8696 lines is a maintainability smell, not a behavioral bug. Splitting it is a refactor — explicitly OUT of scope (boundary: 重构超出 bug 修复范围). Carry rc3 tech-debt.

F7.evidence: readGitChurnWeight no longer exists in doctor.ts; collectEntryPoints (:8266) sorts by path.localeCompare (:8306) — the per-comparison sync git spawn the finding describes was removed. grep: no GitChurn references remain.

F12.evidence: knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs (PreToolUse) is now READ-ONLY: no appendFileSync/writeFileSync/mkdirSync/counter writes remain (grep empty over 1636 lines). The edit-counter/events writes moved to the Stop hook which routes through appendLockedLine. Remaining reads are bounded + inherent to the synchronous hook protocol.

F18.evidence: Stop/broad hooks (fabric-hint.cjs:1293/1503/1528, knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:974) append to events.jsonl/metrics.jsonl via appendLockedLine (advisory-lock; comment :55-57 'appendFileSync can interleave a partial write; route through the advisory-lock'). The narrow hook no longer writes at all. Bare-append interleave addressed.

F19.evidence: iterateCanonicalEntries callers are ALL in doctor.ts (:2975/3073/5791/5818) — a CLI batch command that runs to completion, not the MCP server recall hot path. Sync I/O in a batch CLI does not block a server event loop. (:5634 comment already notes O(N)-per-inspect awareness.)

F21.evidence: effectiveIds.filter + requestedIds.includes is O(N*M) but N,M are candidate/requested-id counts (~dozens at most, bounded by top_k=24). At this scale O(N*M) is negligible; converting to a Set is a micro-opt with no measurable impact. Not a high-severity bug.

F23.evidence: sortDescriptionItems (plan-context.ts:671-694, ISS-006) already uses decorate-sort: scoreDescriptionItem is computed ONCE per item (:686-689) then sorted on the precomputed numeric score. The score-in-comparator antipattern the finding describes (:703-716) no longer exists; comment explicitly documents the prior fix.

F27.evidence: Vector retrieval is OPT-IN (embed_enabled, default OFF — plan-context.ts:362 guard). When off it is a complete no-op (text-only ranking byte-identical). Embedding the corpus per request is the known cost of the opt-in C2 feature; a persisted embedding index is an rc3 enhancement, not a regression in the default path.

F38.evidence: Doctor checks run as a CLI batch command (fabric doctor), not on the MCP server event loop. Synchronous readdirSync/readFileSync/statSync in a to-completion batch tool is acceptable; it does not freeze a long-lived server. Same class as F19.

F42.evidence: 7 per-skill install boilerplate functions are duplication (maintainability), not a behavioral bug. Consolidating into a table-driven loop is a refactor — OUT of scope. Carry rc3.

F43.evidence: isAlive/readLockState duplicated between server legacy-serve-lock-probe.ts and quarantine server-http-experimental serve-lock.ts. Duplication is a maintainability/divergence-risk smell, not a current bug (logic is identical). Refactor — OUT of scope; one side is workspace-excluded quarantine anyway.

F53.evidence: readKnowledgeAcrossStores/aggregatePendingAcrossStores/listStoreKnowledge have NO callers in server/cli src (grep empty) — the multi-store core is unwired scaffolding (v2.1 global-refactor implementation pending). Not reachable on any live hot path; sync I/O there blocks nothing today. Revisit when multi-store is wired.

F56.evidence: GET /api/scan walkFiles is a blocking sync recursive walk on the Express event loop — real, but in @fenglimg/fabric-server-http-experimental (0.0.0-quarantine, private, EXCLUDED from pnpm workspace). Not in rc2 ship surface; defer with F61/F63/F64 until the HTTP server is un-quarantined.
